message:
feat: Embed qjsc into edgeboxc for portability

Makes edgeboxc portable by embedding qjsc functionality instead of requiring
separate qjsc binary. Now edgeboxc can run standalone without full repo structure.

Changes:
- Add QuickJS sources (quickjs.c, libregexp.c, libunicode.c, cutils.c,
  quickjs-libc.c, dtoa.c) to edgeboxc build
- Compile qjsc.c with -Dmain=qjsc_main to avoid symbol conflict
- Create qjsc_wrapper.zig to call qjsc_main() directly instead of subprocess
- Update runtime.zig to use embedded qjsc instead of spawning zig-out/bin/qjsc
- Fix format string error in codegen_ssa.zig (13 args -> 9 args to match placeholders)

Result:
- edgeboxc binary size: 1.7MB -> 2.6MB (+900KB for embedded qjsc)
- No longer needs separate qjsc binary or zig build system at runtime
- Build pipeline works identically (no user-visible changes)

Tested:
- Built bench/hello.js successfully without standalone qjsc binary
- Both bytecode compilation steps (original + hooked) use embedded qjsc
- Build output identical to subprocess approach
